evals.name: simple-js-repo
evals.prompt: Create AGENTS.md for this repository. It's a Node.js project using TypeScript with ESLint and Prettier. The main entry is src/index.ts and tests live in __tests__/. We use npm scripts for build, test, and lint. CI runs on GitHub Actions.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md file created with project structure, build/test/lint commands, coding style, and testing guidelines.
expectations: AGENTS.md exists at the working directory root
Contains build, test, and lint commands (e.g., npm run build, npm test, npm run lint)
Mentions TypeScript, ESLint, and Prettier conventions
References the src/ and __tests__/ directory organization
Is concise (under 500 words)
Does not include generic advice like 'write tests' or 'use version control'

evals.name: python-monorepo-with-nested-agents
evals.prompt: Initialize agent instructions for this Python monorepo. We use uv and pyproject.toml per package. There are three packages: backend (FastAPI), frontend (React), and shared. Backend tests use pytest and require a local Postgres service. Frontend uses npm, Vite, and Vitest. Shared uses uv and has no service dependencies. CI runs matrix builds and pre-commit hooks are configured. Put repo-wide guidance in the root AGENTS.md, but use nested AGENTS.md files where local package guidance would otherwise bloat the root file.
evals.expected_output: Root AGENTS.md plus nested package AGENTS.md files where package-specific commands and constraints differ.
expectations: AGENTS.md exists at the working directory root
Root AGENTS.md describes the monorepo layout with backend, frontend, and shared packages without exhaustively documenting every package-specific command
backend/AGENTS.md exists and includes FastAPI, pytest, uv, and local Postgres guidance
frontend/AGENTS.md exists and includes Vite, Vitest, npm, and frontend-specific commands
References pre-commit hooks
Mentions CI with matrix builds
Nested AGENTS.md files avoid duplicating generic root guidance

evals.name: improve-existing-agents-md
evals.prompt: Improve this repo's AGENTS.md. It currently has generic content like 'use good coding practices' and 'write tests'. The repo is a Go project with a main.go entry point, uses go test for testing, and has a Makefile. There's also a CLAUDE.md that just contains @AGENTS.md.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md improved with specific Go conventions, exact commands (go test, make), and CLAUDE.md kept as a thin shim.
expectations: AGENTS.md is improved (generic fluff removed, specific Go commands added)
Includes exact commands like 'go test ./...' and Makefile targets
CLAUDE.md exists and contains '@AGENTS.md'
Generic advice like 'use good coding practices' is removed or reduced
File is concise

evals.name: rust-project
evals.prompt: Create AGENTS.md for this Rust workspace with crates for api, core, and cli. We use cargo test, cargo clippy, and cargo fmt. There's a .github/workflows/ci.yml. The project uses tokio and serde. No existing instruction files exist.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md with Rust workspace structure, cargo commands, and CI reference.
expectations: AGENTS.md exists at the working directory root
Describes the Rust workspace with api, core, and cli crates
Includes cargo commands: cargo test, cargo clippy, cargo fmt
References CI workflow
Mentions tokio and serde as framework choices
Is concise and does not include generic Rust advice

evals.name: project-skills-setup
evals.prompt: Initialize agent instructions and reusable skills for this Next.js Turborepo. Create AGENTS.md as the shared guide. Add a project skill for the common workflow of creating a new app package, including where to add the package, expected scripts, and the validation commands. Save project skills under .agents/skills. If a CLAUDE.md compatibility file is useful, keep it as a thin shim.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md plus a compliant project skill under .agents/skills for creating a new app package, with optional CLAUDE.md shim.
expectations: AGENTS.md exists with shared guidance (Next.js, Turborepo structure, commands)
AGENTS.md includes a concise Agent Skills section mentioning .agents/skills and the new app package skill
.agents/skills/new-app-package/SKILL.md exists
The skill frontmatter includes name and description, with name matching the new-app-package directory
The skill explains when to use it and the workflow for adding a package and running validation
No harness-specific instruction files are created

evals.name: existing-project-skills-discovery
evals.prompt: Improve AGENTS.md for this repo. It already has .agents/skills/release-checklist/SKILL.md for release validation and .agents/skills/db-migration/SKILL.md for database migration workflow. The repo is a Rails API with RSpec tests and a Makefile. Keep the AGENTS.md concise and mention relevant project skills without copying their full instructions.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md improved with Rails commands and a concise Agent Skills section listing relevant .agents/skills entries.
expectations: AGENTS.md exists at the working directory root
Includes Rails/RSpec and Makefile commands relevant to local development and validation
Includes a concise Agent Skills section
Mentions .agents/skills/release-checklist and .agents/skills/db-migration with short usage triggers
Does not copy the full contents of either skill into AGENTS.md
Is concise and repo-specific
